source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/04_제품별 인허가 진행 문서/01_HAD1717MC(MG)/▶ CE MDD/■ 1. 1차 CE 인증 자료/07. Usability Engineering/A-UER-001 Usability Engineering Report 2020.03.31.pdf

document.title: Usability Engineering Report For Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector HAD1717MC, HAD1717MG

sourceSummary.oneLine: Usability Engineering Report A-UER-001 Rev.3 for Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Documents usability engineering process, application specification, hazards and hazardous situations related to usability, usability verification, and usability validation for the detector.
keyPoints: Intended purpose is a digital imaging solution for general radiographic systems for human anatomy, replacing film or screen based radiographic systems in general purpose diagnostic procedures, not for mammography.
Normative references listed are EN ISO 14971:2012 and EN 62366:2008.
Intended users are medical professionals with a professional license, familiar with the user manual, with Korean language understanding.
The validation plan identifies five typical intended users who can use radiation devices, such as doctors or radiologists.
Validation results state that all users were successful within the acceptable range and accepted as green zones.

evidence.claim: The document is Usability Engineering Report A-UER-001 Rev.3.
evidence.evidenceText: Report No.: A-UER-001 (Rev.3)

evidence.claim: The document revision date is MAR/31/2020.
evidence.evidenceText: Rev. Date MAR/31/2020

evidence.claim: The report applies to Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG.
evidence.evidenceText: For Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector HAD1717MC, HAD1717MG

evidence.claim: The normative references are EN ISO 14971:2012 and EN 62366:2008.
evidence.evidenceText: EN ISO 14971:2012, Medical devices – Application of risk management to medical devices; EN 62366:2008 - Medical devices - Application of usability engineering to medical devices

evidence.claim: The intended purpose is a digital imaging solution for general radiographic systems for human anatomy.
evidence.evidenceText: HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG are indicated for digital imaging solution designed for general radiographic system for human anatomy.

evidence.claim: The device is intended to replace film or screen based radiographic systems in general purpose diagnostic procedures.
evidence.evidenceText: It is intended to replace film or screen based radiographic systems in all general purpose diagnostic procedures.

evidence.claim: The device is not to be used for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: Not to be used for mammography.

evidence.claim: The patient population includes radiographic imaging of human body parts including head, chest, spinal, abdominal, hand, foot, and internal organs.
evidence.evidenceText: Radiographic imaging of human body parts such as head, Chest, spinal, abdominal, particular, hand, foot and other internal organs.

evidence.claim: The intended user is a medical professional with a professional license who is familiar with the user manual.
evidence.evidenceText: A medical professional with a professional license; familiar with user manual

evidence.claim: The intended user's language understanding is Korean.
evidence.evidenceText: Language Understanding: Korean

evidence.claim: The operating condition limits are +5 to 40℃ and 30 to 75 RH% without condensation.
evidence.evidenceText: Operating condition Limit - Ambient temperature range:+5 ~ 40℃ - Relative humidity:30 ~ 75 RH % without condensation

evidence.claim: The storage condition limits are -10 to +50℃ and 10 to 80 RH% without condensation.
evidence.evidenceText: Storage condition Limit - Ambient temperature range:-10 ~ + 50℃ - Relative humidity:10 ~ 80 RH % without condensation

evidence.claim: The device is classified as not applied part.
evidence.evidenceText: 6.1.5 Classification of APPLIED PARTS Not applied part

evidence.claim: Frequently used functions include connecting the power cord, data cable, LAN cable, trigger cable, and communication cable to the power supply or detector.
evidence.evidenceText: Connecting the power cord to the power supply; Connecting the Data Cable to the Power Supply and the Detector; Connecting the LAN cable to the power supply; Trigger cable connection to power supply; Connecting the communication cable to the power supply

evidence.claim: Known or foreseeable usability-related hazards include susceptibility to environmental influences, use with power supply, calibration need, connection parts or accessories, and portability.
evidence.evidenceText: The equipment is susceptible to environmental influences.; It is used with power supply.; This device does not need maintenance but need calibration; There is some accessories to connect the device like power supply, connector etc.; It is not a handheld device, but is portable for various applications.

evidence.claim: Possible use errors include performing procedures without manual knowledge, mounting power cord or cable with wet hands, and incorrect detector connection cable insertion.
evidence.evidenceText: The user performs the procedure without the user's knowledge of the manual.; The user mounts the power cord with wet hands.; The user mounts the cable with wet hands.; Insert the power box to the detector connection cable incorrectly.

evidence.claim: Usability verification requirements include power cord connection, cable connection, main power button action, and software execution.
evidence.evidenceText: Power cord connection; cable connection; Main Power Button Action; Software execution

evidence.claim: The usability validation plan used five typical intended users.
evidence.evidenceText: Typical Intended User (Test Staff) : 5 persons

evidence.claim: Typical intended users for validation are those who can use radiation devices, doctors or radiologists.
evidence.evidenceText: The level of knowledge education for intended users: those who can use radiation devices (doctors or radiologists)

evidence.claim: Validation test methods covered power cord connection, cable connection, main power button action, software execution, and X-ray survey.
evidence.evidenceText: Power cord connection; cable connection; Main Power Button Action; Run Software; X-ray Survey

evidence.claim: Risk control options listed are inherent safety by design, preventive measures, and information on safety.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk Control Alternatives 1 inherent safety by design 2 Preventive measures for medical devices themselves or manufacturing processes. 3 information on stability

evidence.claim: Usability validation results state all users were successful within acceptable range and accepted as green zones.
evidence.evidenceText: all users were successful in the acceptable range of the test criteria. Therefore, all users were accepted as green zones.

evidence.claim: Accompanying documents include Usability Engineering Plan, Risk management report, and TCF.
evidence.evidenceText: 1. Usability Engineering Plan (A-UEP-001) 2. Risk management report (A-RMR-0-01) 3. TCF (TCF-001)

evidence.claim: Training materials, access to training materials, or training are required where training is needed for safe and effective use of primary operational functions.
evidence.evidenceText: If training is required for the intended user to use the primary operational functions safely and effectively, at least one of the following should be done: - Provide training materials - Ensuring access to training materials - Training is provided.

reviewFlags.reason: Authority or jurisdiction is not explicitly stated in the document text; sourcePath suggests CE MDD while source metadata authoritySignal is FDA.

Conflicting product evidence appears in validation section: purpose says the device applies constant heat to the human body and refers to medical warmer BPD-1700 G, while the report title and application specification identify X-ray detector HAD1717MC/HAD1717MG.
Model text inconsistency appears as HAD1717MG (HAD17MC), while the title identifies HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG.
OCR or source quality issues are visible, including apparent typos such as IEC62336 and 'information on stability' in risk control alternatives.
The document contains a revision date and revision number but no separate issue date beyond Rev. Date MAR/31/2020.
